title: Paper resist glaze decoration
notes: Cutting shapes from damp newspaper or newsprint and applying them to a wax-resist-coated or bare bisque surface before dipping or spraying glaze. The paper absorbs the glaze around it; when peeled away before firing, the paper-covered zone has less glaze, creating a two-level glaze depth. Works for geometric or organic shapes. The paper burns away in the kiln. Cone 6.
